<!-- Generated by scripts/build-skills.py from skills/combined/dobby-close-pbi/SKILL.md — do not edit this copy; edit the source and regenerate. -->

Close an Azure DevOps **Product Backlog Item** whose implementation lives in a **GitHub** repository and pull request.

This is the **combined** flow. The split is deliberate, following each tracker's native idiom:

- **Work item state, closing comment, acceptance criteria, dev links → Azure DevOps.** The ADO PBI is the source of truth for status; it is closed explicitly (`State = Done`).
- **Visual evidence → the GitHub PR branch** under `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/`, embedded in the PR description so GitHub renders it inline. The same screenshots are also uploaded to the ADO work item as attachments for the closing comment.

**Input**: A PBI identifier — work item number, `AB#12345`, or an Azure DevOps work item URL. Optionally a summary of what was done.

## Identity check (both trackers)

Verify **both** identities before doing real work so a wrong account is caught early:
```bash
az account show --query "{user:user.name, tenant:tenantId}" --output json
gh auth status
```

## Defaults

Read **both** blocks from `.dobby/config.json`:
- `ado` → `organization`, `project`, `team`, and `devLinks` (see below).
- `github` → `owner`, `repo`.

Both must be populated for combined mode. If either is missing, prompt and persist.

**`ado.devLinks`** controls how commit/branch/PR links attach to the PBI (used in step 7). Since the repo is GitHub, the relevant fields are `repoReachableFromAdo` and, when `true`, `githubConnectionId` (the GUID of the project's Boards ↔ GitHub connection). If `repoReachableFromAdo` is `false` or unknown, links are added as plain **Hyperlinks** (always resolvable) rather than ArtifactLinks.

## ⛔ Command Execution Rules

- **No piping.** Every `az`, `gh`, and `python` command is designed to run standalone with `--output json` / `--json`. Read the full output and extract fields in your own reasoning — no `| jq`, `| Select-Object`, `| grep`, etc.
- **Helper scripts are bundled with the relevant skill** (in its `scripts/` folder) — invoke them exactly as shown in each step.

## Steps

### 1. Validate Prerequisites

- `az version --output json` — `az` present, `azure-devops` extension installed.
- `gh --version` — `gh` present.
- Both identity checks above succeed.
- `git status --porcelain` — if the working tree has uncommitted changes outside `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/`, warn before proceeding.

### 2. Resolve Config

Load the `ado` and `github` blocks. Validate ADO access and GitHub repo access (`gh repo view "<owner>/<repo>" --json name`).

### 3. Find the PBI (Azure DevOps)

Parse the input (numeric id, `AB#<id>`, ADO URL, or an OpenSpec change name containing `pbi-<id>`):
```bash
az boards work-item show --id <id> --organization "<org-url>" --output json
```
Extract title, state, type, description, acceptance criteria, child tasks. **If already Done**, offer to add closing remarks instead of failing.

### 4. Find the GitHub PR

The PR is the implementation + evidence vehicle. Find the open PR for the implementation branch:
```bash
gh pr list --repo "<owner>/<repo>" --state open --json number,title,body,headRefName,url --limit 50
```
Match by the implementation branch (often `feat/<id>-*` or `fix/<id>-*`) or by an `AB#<id>` reference in the PR body. If multiple match, ask the user to confirm. Record the PR number, URL, branch (`headRefName`), and head commit SHA.

> Note: GitHub's `Closes #N` only closes **GitHub issues**, not ADO PBIs — so this skill closes the PBI explicitly in step 8. The PR is for evidence and dev-link traceability.

### 5. Gather Evidence

Classify the change (Bug/UI → before+after; new UI → after only; non-UI → none) using the same signals as the standard close flows. For UI changes, prefer Playwright when the project supports it; output PNGs to `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/{before,after}-*.png`.

**5a. Commit evidence to the GitHub PR branch:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout <headRefName>
git pull --ff-only
git add docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/
git commit -m "docs(evidence): screenshots for PBI <id>"
git push origin <headRefName>
```
Stage **only** `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/`. Push before updating the PR description so embedded image URLs resolve.

**5b. Embed evidence in the PR description:** update the PR body with `### Before` / `### After` sections referencing `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/*.png` (repo-relative paths). Preserve user-authored content:
```bash
gh pr edit <pr-number> --repo "<owner>/<repo>" --body-file <path-to-body.md>
```

### 6. Compose the ADO Closing Comment

Build the closing comment for the PBI Discussion. Include a `### Source` section pointing at the **GitHub** commit/branch/PR, and `### Before` / `### After` sections. Use **local file path placeholders** for images first; preview to the user; do not upload yet. If cancelled, abort without changing state.

**6a. Upload screenshots to ADO** (so the comment renders inline on the work item):
```bash
python dobby-close-pbi/scripts/azdo-upload-attachment.py \
    --work-item-id <id> --org "<org-url>" --project "<project>" \
    <file1> <file2> ...
```
Replace the placeholders with the returned ADO attachment URLs.

**6b. Post the comment** (markdown-formatted via the history patch):
```bash
python dobby-close-pbi/scripts/azdo-add-comment.py \
    --work-item-id <id> --org "<org-url>" --project "<project>" \
    --file <path-to-comment.md>
```
**Never** use `az boards work-item update --discussion` for markdown — it stores HTML. To edit, delete (`dobby-close-pbi/scripts/azdo-delete-comment.py`) and re-post.

### 7. Add Development Links (GitHub commit / branch / PR → ADO PBI)

Link the GitHub commit, branch, and PR to the PBI. Link type depends on `ado.devLinks.repoReachableFromAdo`:
```bash
python dobby-close-pbi/scripts/azdo-add-dev-links.py \
    --work-item-id <id> --org "<org-url>" --project "<project>" \
    --commit-url "https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/commit/<full-sha>" --commit-comment "<subject> (<short-sha>)" \
    --branch-url "https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/tree/<branch>" --branch-comment "Implementation branch" \
    --pr-url "<pr-url>" --pr-comment "PR #<pr-number>" \
    [--gh-connection-id <guid>]
```
With `--gh-connection-id` (and `repoReachableFromAdo: true`) the commit/PR attach as ArtifactLinks; otherwise as Hyperlinks. Use the **full 40-char SHA**. Push the branch first so the URLs resolve.

### 8. Acceptance Criteria & Close (Azure DevOps)

**8a. Check off acceptance criteria** against the evidence — change confirmed `- [ ]` to `- [x]`; leave unconfirmed unchecked and list them in the closing comment:
```bash
python dobby-create-pbi/scripts/azdo-update-fields.py \
    --work-item-id <id> --org "<org-url>" --project "<project>" \
    --field "Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria=<path-to-ac.md>"
```

**8b. Child tasks**: list them; with user confirmation, set open children to Done.

**8c. Set state to Done:**
```bash
az boards work-item update --id <id> --state "Done" --organization "<org-url>" --output json
```
If a workflow rule requires an intermediate state, transition through it first.

**8d. Archive the OpenSpec change** (optional) if one was found: `openspec archive "<change-name>"`.

### 9. Display Result

```
## ✓ PBI Closed (combined)

- **PBI**: #<id> — "<title>"  [Done]
- **PR**:  #<pr-number> — <pr-url>  (evidence committed + embedded)
- **Dev links**: commit / branch / PR linked to the PBI
- **Acceptance criteria**: N/N checked
- **Screenshots**: N committed to docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/ and attached to the PBI
- **PBI URL**: <work-item-url>
```

## Guardrails

- Verify **both** identities early.
- Close the **PBI** explicitly (`State = Done`) — GitHub's `Closes` does not touch ADO work items.
- Never `git add .` / `git add -A` — stage only `docs/evidence/pbi-<id>/`. Never amend or force-push.
- Push the PR branch before embedding images or adding dev links, so URLs resolve.
- Use clean markdown in ADO comments (no HTML, no `<!-- -->`); omit empty sections.
- Verify acceptance criteria against evidence — do not blindly check every box.
- Show the closing comment and PR-body changes to the user before posting.

## Usage Examples

> Close PBI 1021108 — implemented the language toggle, PR is up

> Wrap up AB#1021108, screenshots already in docs/evidence/pbi-1021108/
